title: Product Design Harness Verdict Override
description: Authorizes execution despite a verify or stop_reframe verdict. An override never modifies the review-result: the review's verdict stays on record, and this artifact records who chose to execute anyway, within what scope, until when, and what voids the authorization. Validate with scripts/check_override.py, which also cross-checks the referenced review.

override_id.description: Stable identifier; any document that references the overridden review must carry it.

review_id.description: The review_id of the review-result being overridden.

overridden_verdict.description: Must equal the review's combined_verdict. A continue verdict needs no override.

acknowledged_weakest_flow.description: Must equal the review's weakest_flow; the owner restates the gap they are accepting.

authorized_scope.description: Execution scope granted despite the verdict. No may_do entry may repeat an entry from the review's must_not_do.

accountable_owner.description: A named person, not a team.

rationale.description: Why executing despite the verdict is the right call.

expires_at.description: Every override is time-boxed. When it lapses, the original verdict is in force again.

tripwires.description: Measurable conditions; any one tripping voids the override immediately.

renewal_requires.description: Evidence a renewal review must present. Renewal is a new review, never an edit.
